title: [phase:simulate] [family:canary-migration] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux replacement. Simulate a canary-ready migration path from openclaw-tmux to the wrapper prototype. Return concise bullets: first canary workflow, stop conditions, rollback triggers, manifest/doc migration orde...
summary_text: - **First canary workflow** - Ship wrapper as `opt-in` behind `TMUX_WRAPPER_CANARY=1`; keep `openclaw-tmux` as default. - Run **shadow mode** first: wrapper executes read-only/session-discovery paths in parallel, decisions still come from `openclaw-tmux`. - Gate promotion on parity: command resolution, pane targeting, exit code, and stdout shape must match baseline. - Move to **write-path canary** for a small cohort (e.g., 5%), then 25%, then 100% after stable windows. - Keep dual-lane verification active during canary (execution lane + independent parity/audit lane). - **Stop conditions** - Any contract mismatch in session selection, pane index, or command dispatch. - Error-rate or timeout regression above agreed SLO (e.g., +1% absolute failures or +20% p95 latency). - Missing/invalid audit events for any mutating operation. - Loss of required parallel verification lane (single-lane fallback detected). - **Rollback triggers** - One Sev1 incident or two consecutive Sev2 incidents attributable to wrapper path. - Parity failure on mutating command categories (send-keys, capture-pane, session attach/kill). - Canary health check fails for 2 consecutive windows. - On trigger: flip feature flag to `0`, force route to `openclaw-tmux`, preserve canary logs/artifacts for postmortem. - **Manifest/doc migration order** - Add wrapper entry to manifest as `disabled-by-default` with explicit fallback target. - Add routing policy docs: canary gates, stop conditions, rollback command/flag. - Update operator runbook/checklist (preflight, parity checks, incident path). - Update skill/docs/examples to prefer wrapper flag path while documenting legacy fallback. - After stable soak, switch default manifest route to wrapper; then deprecate legacy docs; finally remove legacy manifest entry.
